info.name: Newton Judge0 — All Languages (0.33)
info.description:
End-to-end smoke for every active language id in db/languages/active.rb.
Mirrors bin/newton-smoke-test as importable Postman requests.

Usage:
  1. Import this file in Postman.
  2. Edit the collection variable `base_url` to point at your environment
     (e.g. http://localhost:2358 for dev, https://judge0.newtonschool.co for prod).
  3. Run individual requests, or use the Collection Runner to execute all.

All requests use `?wait=true` so the response is the final submission result
(no polling loop). Each request has a Tests script that asserts:
  - HTTP 201
  - status.description === "Accepted"
  - stdout contains "Hello, Newton!"

If a language fails, tweak `judge0.conf` knobs (memory/processes/file-size/time)
and re-run. The full conf is documented at the top of judge0.conf.

item.name: .NET (archived in 0.33)
item.description: C# (51) and F# (87) are archived because .NET CoreCLR is incompatible with isolate's rlimit-mode resource limits (RLIMIT_AS breaks GC heap init; RLIMIT_FSIZE triggers SIGXFSZ on JIT writes). Will be revived once isolate cgroup-mode lands. The requests are kept here so prod can re-test once unarchived.

body.raw:
{
  "language_id": 77,
  "source_code": "       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. Hello.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
           DISPLAY "Hello, Newton!".
           STOP RUN."
}

{
  "language_id": 45,
  "source_code": "section .data
    msg db "Hello, Newton!", 10
    len equ $ - msg
section .text
global _start
_start:
    mov rax, 1
    mov rdi, 1
    mov rsi, msg
    mov rdx, len
    syscall
    mov rax, 60
    xor rdi, rdi
    syscall"
}

{
  "language_id": 3001,
  "source_code": "        .data
msg:    .asciiz "Hello, Newton!\n"
        .text
main:
        li      $v0, 4
        la      $a0, msg
        syscall
        li      $v0, 10
        syscall"
}

item.name: Jack (3002) — VM with screen output, not stdout
